reasoning: The document contains a critical programmatic defect due to pervasive header and footer artifacts retained in the HTML output across all 5 pages. Additionally, there are major defects identified including a missing meaningful digital signature image and mixed numbering types in lists indicating sub-point flattening. According to the count-based severity rules, the presence of a critical defect requires automatic rejection.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5, pages_affected=5/5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

[Downgraded: image_count_ratio=1.00, placeholder_ratio=0.00 contradict LLM claim] The first page of the source PDF contains a prominent digital signature stamp and cursive signature image ('Elizabeth S Werner') next to the 'From:' line. This is a meaningful image that establishes the authenticity and authorization of the memorandum. There is no corresponding image (or transcribed text of the digital stamp) present in the HTML output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Cursive signature image and digital signature stamp text ('Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner...')
concerns.output_quote: The signature image and stamp are completely absent from the HTML output.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p5:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

In the source PDF, the run-in headers for the bulleted list items on pages 3, 4, and 5 are both bolded and underlined (e.g., 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments', 'Beneficial Reuse'). In the extraction JSON, these terms are presented as plain text without any markdown or HTML indicating bold or underline emphasis. This is a clear loss of structural/visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Excavation – Speakers requested that full excavation occur...
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Excavation – Speakers requested that full excavation occur as quickly as possible."
concerns.location: Sections II and III (Pages 3-5 bulleted lists)

action_items: Remove pervasive header and footer artifacts from the HTML extraction
Ensure digital signatures and meaningful images are retained or properly described
Fix flattened list structures to ensure proper nested numbering
Restore visual emphasis (bold/underline) on run-in headers
